body.title: Shim as persistent front-door on the Ground Loop: Granny wakes-not-launches; availability = wakeable; stall -> alarm/park
body.text:
CAPTURED 2026-07-02 (live design session). Makes the worker a DORMANT personality behind a PERSISTENT front-door, and retires the last Granny launch path. Extends the rule already set for CC ('Granny must NEVER subprocess-spawn a CC' — D-cc-shim-assignment-model-2026-06-06; granny/dispatch.py is RETIRED and raises) to DickSimnel, the one surviving launch_cmd.

THE KEYSTONE (Akien): shims ATTACH TO THE GROUND LOOP for daemon lifetime + MCP receive (skeleton is the MCP aggregator). So a worker's shim is ALIVE even when its heavy device is down — no new per-worker supervisor to build; the Ground Loop already exists as the persistent supervisor ([[project_ground_loop_web_server]]). The shim's lifecycle BIFURCATES:
  - FRONT-DOOR half (attached to the Ground Loop, always up): holds the availability flag, receives Granny's MCP wake-call via skeleton, LEASES instance numbers (owns the reusable free-list, 0=foreground), and spawns/wakes the device.
  - DEVICE half (the personality): heavy, dormant, spun up + torn down by its own front-door. Restarts freely — all durable state (flag, number lease) lives in the front-door ([[feedback_external_state_principle]]).

'THE LAUNCH MOVED, IT DIDN'T DISAPPEAR': Granny does not launch; the worker's OWN front-door wakes the device on request. Authority to start DS.0 moves from Granny (granny.yaml launch_cmd) to DS.0's shim.

AVAILABILITY FLAG SEMANTICS SHIFT: from 'device is running and listening' to 'worker is WAKEABLE (front-door up)'. That is what Granny's MRU reads. (Today DickSimnelShim.start() launches the listener INSIDE the device process — when DS.0 is down nothing listens; this decision moves the listening front-door onto the Ground Loop so it survives the device.)

GRANNY BEHAVIOR (Akien's narrative, POV): ticket waiting -> any builder available? -> consult MRU of wakeable front-doors ('that nice boy DS.0 was by yesterday') -> MCP-call DS.0's front-door (device currently DOWN) -> front-door sees no reason DS.0 can't wake, replies 'i'm on it!', wakes DS.0 + hands off the ticket ('happens by magic'). If DS.0 can't be woken AND nobody else is available -> system_alarm ('I'M STALLED! HELP!'); Granny's settings-panel shows STALLED + a RESUME button; she PARKS, not spins forever (anti-infinite-cycle). Fits 'an alarm = anything Akien and Claude must look at together' (D-system-alarms-and-tier-requests).

CONCRETE CASUALTY: granny.yaml DS.0 launch_cmd is REMOVED (+ daemon.py 'only DickSimnel.0 is static (needs launch_cmd)' static-worker handling). Low-stakes to pull early since DS is off until free inference ([[project_ds_cost_constraint]]) — but the removal is only SAFE once the front-door wake path exists (else DS.0 has no start path). GATE the removal on the wake protocol.

OPEN QUESTIONS (resolve before spawning the downstream build tickets):
  - Q1: one Ground Loop per HOST hosting all worker shims (assumed) vs a Ground Loop per shim.
  - Q2: the shim<->Ground-Loop attach interface — how a shim registers for daemon lifetime + an MCP receive endpoint (via skeleton aggregator).
  - Q3: the wake protocol shape — MCP verb, the 'i'm on it' ack, spawn mechanics, and the sleep/teardown trigger (idle timeout? explicit?).

DOWNSTREAM TICKETS (file after Q1-Q3 reconcile; NOT filed now to avoid hollow build tickets on an undesigned interface):
  - T-shim-lease-instance-numbers (M): front-door owns the reusable free-list (0=foreground); instance asks at __init__ (wires D-worker-instance-identity's leasing). Gated on T-shim-frontdoor-on-groundloop.
  - T-granny-wake-not-launch (M): Granny routes wake via the front-door MCP call; REMOVE DS.0 launch_cmd from granny.yaml + daemon static-worker handling. Gated on the wake protocol.
  - T-granny-mru-and-stall-alarm (M): Granny keeps an MRU of wakeable front-doors; no-wakeable + none-available -> system_alarm + stalled state + resume button. Links the already-filed alarm tickets (T-alarm-callsites-groundloop-db-install, T-vetinari-owns-alarm-escalation). Gated on T-granny-wake-not-launch.

## Hypothesis
A worker survives device restarts as a dormant personality behind an always-up front-door; Granny never launches — she calls, the front-door wakes; and a rack with no wakeable worker PARKS on a system_alarm instead of cycling.

## Intention
I intend that Granny never launches a worker — the worker's own persistent shim (attached to the Ground Loop) is the sole authority that wakes its dormant device on request.

RELATES TO: D-worker-instance-identity-2026-07-02, [[project_ground_loop_web_server]], skeleton (MCP aggregator), D-system-alarms-and-tier-requests, [[feedback_granny_no_cc_spawn]], [[feedback_external_state_principle]].

=== RESOLVED 2026-07-02 (live design session) ===
Q1 (topology): ONE Ground Loop per HOST — but REFRAMED: the Ground Loop does NOT 'supervise' in any real sense. It is a DUMB daemon loop that relaunches runme.py files (if the shim allows) and CHECKS NOTHING. All supervision/checking is in the shim class, never the Ground Loop.
Q2 (attach): runme.py PLUGIN — the shim ships a runme.py the existing RunmeSupervisor already loads, supervises, and borkedits on failure. NO register()/registry — that would violate the GL-stays-as-simple-as-possible intention. The shim + its runme.py IS the device's supervisor. TWO-LEVEL model: GL <-> front-door (relaunch the runme.py); front-door <-> device (wake on work, idle-sleep). It is the web-proxy pattern GENERALIZED — the web server is the DEVICE, the SHIM is the proxy; the daemon loop keeps the proxy ready; the proxy wakes the web server on an incoming request when it is down.
Q3 (wake/sleep): idle TIMEOUT. Wake fires as a side-effect of work arriving (the proxy pattern); the device idle-sleeps after a config timeout, detected by the shim BUS-ASKING each instance 'whatcha workin' on?'. The full leasing / pool / singleton / idle-sleep mechanics are specified in D-worker-pool-leasing-and-singleton-2026-07-02.
DOWNSTREAM TICKETS NOW FILED (Qs resolved -> no longer open_questions): T-shim-lease-instance-numbers, T-worker-pool-semantics, T-worker-idle-sleep-bus-ask, T-granny-wake-not-launch, T-granny-mru-and-stall-alarm. The capability-TAG routing convergence is DEFERRED under D-unified-capability-selector-deferred-2026-07-02 (ticket converges 'when more of the restructuring is done').

text: REFINEMENT (2026-07-02, T-granny-wake-not-launch build): T-granny-wake's description said Granny wakes a worker by CALLING its front-door via MCP (skeleton) and getting a synchronous 'i'm on it' ack. Built instead as BUS-DISPATCH-WAKES: Granny dispatches to the worker's mailbox (dicksimnel.0) exactly as she does for any available worker; the Ground-Loop-managed front-door idle_waits that mailbox and wakes the device on arrival (this ticket's proven mechanism). Rationale: (1) this front-door design — 'the proxy wakes the web server when a request comes in' — is what Akien drove in Q1 and is already proven; the MCP-call phrasing was the pre-front-door sketch and the resolved+proven mechanism wins; (2) an MCP wake endpoint would be a SECOND wake path beside idle_wait, breaking the single-mechanism discipline (YAGNI, no caller — verified by grep: no skeleton/granny consumer expects a front-door wake tool); (3) Granny's handshake is already ASYNC (does not wait for ack), so the device's existing dispatch_ack IS 'i'm on it', just after wake latency. No synchronous-ack contract is broken. Orphaned-front-door detection stays deferred to T-granny-mru-and-stall-alarm; the existing stale-dispatched watchdog escalates un-acked work to CC meanwhile, so a down front-door is not a silent drop.
